channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina dies at 71
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham died Saturday at the age of 71. The South Carolina Republican was a committed advocate of U.S. military intervention abroad and a close ally of President Trump.
item.content:encoded: Sen. Lindsey Graham died Saturday at the age of 71. The South Carolina Republican was a committed advocate of U.S. military intervention abroad and a close ally of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Sen. Richard Blumenthal talks about Lindsey Graham's legacy
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Sen. Richard Blumenthal, D-Conn., about Sen. Lindsey Graham's legacy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Sen. Richard Blumenthal, D-Conn., about Sen. Lindsey Graham's legacy.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Houston community holds vigil for man killed by ICE agents
item.description: A mix of grief and outrage was on display over the weekend at a vigil for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who was fatally shot in Houston by ICE agents.
item.content:encoded: A mix of grief and outrage was on display over the weekend at a vigil for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who was fatally shot in Houston by ICE agents.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Seward

item.title: A guard punched him on camera. It was still nearly impossible for him to sue
item.description: Prisoners are allowed to file complaints about mistreatment — but doing so often comes with retaliation. That's according to an investigation by NPR and The Marshall Project.
item.content:encoded: Prisoners are allowed to file complaints about mistreatment — but doing so often comes with retaliation. That's according to an investigation by NPR and The Marshall Project.
item.dc:creator: Christie Thompson

item.title: Eat your ice cream for a long, healthy life? This doctor says so
item.description: Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.

(Image credit: WeBond Creations)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The U.S. strikes Iran for a third weekend as Iran retaliates by hitting Gulf nations, Sen. Lindsey Graham died Saturday at the age of 71, Congress returns from recess this week.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. strikes Iran for a third weekend as Iran retaliates by hitting Gulf nations, Sen. Lindsey Graham died Saturday at the age of 71, Congress returns from recess this week.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Maine Democrats scramble to select replacement for Platner in Senate race
item.description: Maine Democrats now have a process for choosing a replacement for Senate candidate Graham Platner. But voters are still reeling from his fall.
item.content:encoded: Maine Democrats now have a process for choosing a replacement for Senate candidate Graham Platner. But voters are still reeling from his fall.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Maine Secretary of State Shenna Bellows on her bid to replace Platner in Senate race
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Maine Secretary of State Shenna Bellows about her run to replace Graham Platner in the Maine Senate race.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Maine Secretary of State Shenna Bellows about her run to replace Graham Platner in the Maine Senate race.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Palestinian doctor in Israeli custody is in critical condition, his lawyer says
item.description: A Palestinian doctor detained for more than 550 days in Israeli prison is close to death after near daily beatings, says his lawyer.
item.content:encoded: A Palestinian doctor detained for more than 550 days in Israeli prison is close to death after near daily beatings, says his lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: A recap of the Wimbledon finals and what to expect at the US Open
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Scooby Axson of USA Today Sports about this year's men's and women's finals at Wimbledon and a look ahead to the US Open.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Scooby Axson of USA Today Sports about this year's men's and women's finals at Wimbledon and a look ahead to the US Open.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
